speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Three thousand ducats, well.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Ay, sir, for three months.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: For three months, well.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: For the which, as I told you, Antonio shall

speaker: BASSANIO
text: be bound.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Antonio shall become bound, well.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: May you stead me? Will you pleasure me?

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Shall I know your answer?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Three thousand ducats for three months,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: and Antonio bound.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Your answer to that?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Antonio is a good man.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Have you heard any imputation to the

speaker: BASSANIO
text: contrary?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Ho, no, no, no, no! My meaning in saying he

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: is a good man is to have you understand me that he

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: is sufficient. Yet his means are in supposition: he

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: hath an argosy bound to Tripolis, another to the

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Indies. I understand, moreover, upon the Rialto,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: he hath a third at Mexico, a fourth for England, and

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: other ventures he hath squandered abroad. But

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: ships are but boards, sailors but men; there be land

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: rats and water rats, water thieves and land

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: thieves—I mean pirates—and then there is the

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: peril of waters, winds, and rocks. The man is,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: notwithstanding, sufficient. Three thousand ducats.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I think I may take his bond.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Be assured you may.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I will be assured I may. And that I may be

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: assured, I will bethink me. May I speak with

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Antonio?

speaker: BASSANIO
text: If it please you to dine with us.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Yes, to smell pork! To eat of the habitation

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: which your prophet the Nazarite conjured the

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: devil into! I will buy with you, sell with you, talk

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: with you, walk with you, and so following; but I

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: will not eat with you, drink with you, nor pray with

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: you.—What news on the Rialto?—Who is he comes

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: here?

speaker: BASSANIO
text: This is Signior Antonio.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: How like a fawning publican he looks!

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I hate him for he is a Christian,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: But more for that in low simplicity

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: He lends out money gratis and brings down

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: The rate of usance here with us in Venice.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: If I can catch him once upon the hip,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I will feed fat the ancient grudge I bear him.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: He hates our sacred nation, and he rails,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Even there where merchants most do congregate,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: On me, my bargains, and my well-won thrift,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Which he calls “interest.” Cursèd be my tribe

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: If I forgive him!

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Shylock, do you hear?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I am debating of my present store,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And, by the near guess of my memory,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I cannot instantly raise up the gross

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Of full three thousand ducats. What of that?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Tubal, a wealthy Hebrew of my tribe,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Will furnish me. But soft, how many months

speaker: SHYLOCK
text:
Do you desire?
                 
                To
                 
                Antonio
                .
               Rest you fair, good signior!

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Your Worship was the last man in our mouths.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Shylock, albeit I neither lend nor borrow

speaker: ANTONIO
text: By taking nor by giving of excess,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Yet, to supply the ripe wants of my friend,

speaker: ANTONIO
text:
I’ll break a custom.
                 
                To
                 
                Bassanio
                .
               Is he yet possessed

speaker: ANTONIO
text: How much you would?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Ay, ay, three thousand ducats.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: And for three months.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text:
I had forgot—three months.
                 
                To
                 
                Bassanio
                .
              You told me so.—

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Well then, your bond. And let me see—but hear you:

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Methoughts you said you neither lend nor borrow

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Upon advantage.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: I do never use it.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: When Jacob grazed his Uncle Laban’s sheep—

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: This Jacob from our holy Abram was

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: (As his wise mother wrought in his behalf)

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: The third possessor; ay, he was the third—

speaker: ANTONIO
text: And what of him? Did he take interest?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: No, not take interest, not, as you would say,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Directly “interest.” Mark what Jacob did.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: When Laban and himself were compromised

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: That all the eanlings which were streaked and pied

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Should fall as Jacob’s hire, the ewes being rank

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: In end of autumn turnèd to the rams,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And when the work of generation was

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Between these woolly breeders in the act,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: The skillful shepherd pilled me certain wands,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And in the doing of the deed of kind

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: He stuck them up before the fulsome ewes,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Who then conceiving did in eaning time

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Fall parti-colored lambs, and those were Jacob’s.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: This was a way to thrive, and he was blest;

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And thrift is blessing if men steal it not.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: This was a venture, sir, that Jacob served for,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: A thing not in his power to bring to pass,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: But swayed and fashioned by the hand of heaven.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Was this inserted to make interest good?

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Or is your gold and silver ewes and rams?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I cannot tell; I make it breed as fast.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: But note me, signior—

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Mark you this, Bassanio,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: The devil can cite Scripture for his purpose!

speaker: ANTONIO
text: An evil soul producing holy witness

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Is like a villain with a smiling cheek,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: A goodly apple rotten at the heart.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: O, what a goodly outside falsehood hath!

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Three thousand ducats. ’Tis a good round sum.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Three months from twelve, then let me see, the rate—

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Well, Shylock, shall we be beholding to you?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Signior Antonio, many a time and oft

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: In the Rialto you have rated me

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: About my moneys and my usances.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Still have I borne it with a patient shrug

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: (For suff’rance is the badge of all our tribe).

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: You call me misbeliever, cutthroat dog,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And spet upon my Jewish gaberdine,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And all for use of that which is mine own.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Well then, it now appears you need my help.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Go to, then. You come to me and you say

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: “Shylock, we would have moneys”—you say so,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: You, that did void your rheum upon my beard,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And foot me as you spurn a stranger cur

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Over your threshold. Moneys is your suit.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: What should I say to you? Should I not say

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: “Hath a dog money? Is it possible

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: A cur can lend three thousand ducats?” Or

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Shall I bend low, and in a bondman’s key,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: With bated breath and whisp’ring humbleness,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Say this: “Fair sir, you spet on me on Wednesday last;

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: You spurned me such a day; another time

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: You called me ‘dog’; and for these courtesies

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I’ll lend you thus much moneys”?

speaker: ANTONIO
text: I am as like to call thee so again,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: To spet on thee again, to spurn thee, too.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: If thou wilt lend this money, lend it not

speaker: ANTONIO
text: As to thy friends, for when did friendship take

speaker: ANTONIO
text: A breed for barren metal of his friend?

speaker: ANTONIO
text: But lend it rather to thine enemy,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Who, if he break, thou mayst with better face

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Exact the penalty.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Why, look you how you storm!

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I would be friends with you and have your love,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Forget the shames that you have stained me with,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Supply your present wants, and take no doit

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Of usance for my moneys, and you’ll not hear me!

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: This is kind I offer.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: This were kindness!

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: This kindness will I show.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Go with me to a notary, seal me there

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Your single bond; and in a merry sport,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: If you repay me not on such a day,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: In such a place, such sum or sums as are

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Expressed in the condition, let the forfeit

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Be nominated for an equal pound

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Of your fair flesh, to be cut off and taken

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: In what part of your body pleaseth me.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Content, in faith. I’ll seal to such a bond,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: And say there is much kindness in the Jew.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: You shall not seal to such a bond for me!

speaker: BASSANIO
text: I’ll rather dwell in my necessity.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Why, fear not, man, I will not forfeit it!

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Within these two months—that’s a month before

speaker: ANTONIO
text: This bond expires—I do expect return

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Of thrice three times the value of this bond.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: O father Abram, what these Christians are,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Whose own hard dealings teaches them suspect

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: The thoughts of others! Pray you tell me this:

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: If he should break his day, what should I gain

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: By the exaction of the forfeiture?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: A pound of man’s flesh taken from a man

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Is not so estimable, profitable neither,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: As flesh of muttons, beefs, or goats. I say,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: To buy his favor I extend this friendship.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: If he will take it, so. If not, adieu;

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And for my love I pray you wrong me not.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Yes, Shylock, I will seal unto this bond.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Then meet me forthwith at the notary’s.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Give him direction for this merry bond,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And I will go and purse the ducats straight,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: See to my house left in the fearful guard

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Of an unthrifty knave, and presently

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I’ll be with you.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Hie thee, gentle Jew.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: The Hebrew will turn Christian; he grows kind.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: I like not fair terms and a villain’s mind.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Come on, in this there can be no dismay;

speaker: ANTONIO
text: My ships come home a month before the day.
